verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Want of room the cause, precedent the justification.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Believe me, Sire, and let Truth be my witness in this also, when I declare that in the congregations held during the season of Lent, in consequence of the narrow limits of the places, and the vast multitude of people assembled, a great number of children, not a few of the younger and very many of the older women, besides several young men, suffered so much from the pressure of the crowd, that they were obliged to be carried home; though by the Providence of God, no one is dead.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: All however murmured, and demanded the use of the great Church.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: And if the pressure was so great during the days which preceded the feast, what would have been the case during the feast itself?

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Of course matters would have been far worse.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: It did not therefore become me to change the people’s joy into grief, their cheerfulness into sorrow, and to make the festival a season of lamentation.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: And that the more, because I had a precedent in the conduct of our Fathers.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: For the blessed Alexander, when the other places were too small, and he was engaged in the erection of what was then considered a very large one, the Church of Theonas, held his congregations there on account of the number of the people, while at the same time he proceeded with the building.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: I have seen the same thing done at Treveri and at Aquileia, in both which places, while the building was proceeding, they assembled there during the feasts, on account of the number of the people and they never found any one to accuse them in this manner.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: Nay, your brother of blessed memory was present, when a communion was held under these circumstances at Aquileia.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: There was no dedication, but only a service of prayer.

verses.book: Apology to the Emperor. (Apologia Ad Constantium.)
verses.text: You, at least I am sure, as a lover of God will approve of the people’s zeal, and will pardon me for being unwilling to hinder the prayers of so great a multitude.
